title: toxicodendron radicans var. eximium
intro: Toxicodendron radicans var. eximium, also known as poison ivy, is a highly allergenic and toxic plant native to eastern North America. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as eczema, acne, and skin irritation. The health benefits of poison ivy are primarily derived from its sap, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been shown to reduce pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of poison ivy are typically made from the plant's leaves and stems, and can be found in the form of tinctures, salves, and ointments.
